title: erigeron alpinus subsp. villarsii
intro: Erigeron alpinus subsp. villarsii, also known as Alpine Fleabane, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the high mountains of Europe. The medicinal properties of Erigeron alpinus subsp. villarsii include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall well-being. Erigeron alpinus subsp. villarsii can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
